question: What are the items you must sign in at the END of your shift?
options: Pillows, duvet and duvet cover
Keycard, room attendant's report and walkie talkie
Guests' lost and found items
Dental kit, sanitary bag and soap
correctAnswer: Keycard, room attendant's report and walkie talkie
hint: Think about what you were given at the START of your shift. These hotel items must be returned.
explanation: At the end of your shift, you sign in the keycard, room attendant's report and walkie talkie. These are hotel equipment issued to you at the start of the shift and must be returned and accounted for.

question: Are you allowed to help your colleagues sign out their work items for them?
options: Yes, it is important to be helpful
Yes, as long as my supervisor does not know about it
No, my colleagues did not help me sign out my items
No, it is against company rules to sign out work items for colleagues
correctAnswer: No, it is against company rules to sign out work items for colleagues
hint: Each person must be responsible for their own items. Signing out for someone else creates accountability problems.
explanation: It is against company rules to sign out work items for colleagues. Every staff member must personally sign out their own items so they are accountable for them.

question: Which items are required for cleaning and servicing guestrooms?
options: Cleaning chemicals in caddy, walkie talkie, and dirty towels
Cleaning chemicals in caddy, dirty towels, cleaning cloths, and rubber gloves
Cleaning chemicals in caddy, cleaning cloths, rubber gloves, and new guest supplies
All items including walkie talkie, dirty towels, cleaning cloths, and rubber gloves
correctAnswer: Cleaning chemicals in caddy, cleaning cloths, rubber gloves, and new guest supplies
hint: You need cleaning supplies, protective gloves, and fresh items for guests. A walkie talkie is for communication, not cleaning.
explanation: The correct items are cleaning chemicals in a caddy, cleaning cloths, rubber gloves, and new guest supplies. A walkie talkie is communication equipment, and dirty towels are collected from rooms — neither are cleaning tools you bring to service a room.

question: You see a 'Do Not Disturb' sign on the guest's door. What do you do?
options: Press the doorbell
Wait for the guest to open the door
Enter the room and wake the guest up
Go to the next room shown on your room attendant's report
correctAnswer: Go to the next room shown on your room attendant's report
hint: The DND sign means the guest does NOT want to be disturbed at all. Skip this room.
explanation: When you see a 'Do Not Disturb' sign, you must respect the guest's privacy. Go to the next room on your room attendant's report. Never ring the doorbell, wait, or enter a room with a DND sign.

question: It is past the guest's checkout time of 3pm. The 'Do Not Disturb' sign is still on the door. What do you do?
options: Inform your supervisor
Press the doorbell and inform the guest to check out
Press the doorbell and scold the guest for not checking out
Go to the next room shown on your room attendant's report
correctAnswer: Inform your supervisor
hint: Checkout time has passed — this is unusual. You should not handle this situation alone. Who do you report to?
explanation: When checkout time has passed but the DND sign is still up, inform your supervisor immediately. Do not attempt to scold the guest or handle this alone — it requires a supervisor's decision.

question: Which of these describes a clean and well-presented hotel room?
options: Bed with rumpled sheets and pillows scattered around
Neatly made bed, smooth sheets, pillows in place, tidy room
Unmade bed with sheets hanging off the sides
Bed covered with the guest's personal belongings
correctAnswer: Neatly made bed, smooth sheets, pillows in place, tidy room
hint: A well-presented room looks neat, tidy, and ready for the guest to enjoy.
explanation: A clean and well-presented hotel room has a neatly made bed with smooth sheets, pillows properly placed, and everything tidy and ready for the guest.

question: Which statement about a clean and well-presented room is CORRECT?
options: It makes guests feel happy, comfortable and safe
It attracts more guests to stay in the hotel
It shows that staff are professional and doing a good job
All of the above
correctAnswer: All of the above
hint: Think about everyone who benefits — the current guest, future guests, and the staff.
explanation: All three statements are correct. A clean room makes guests feel happy, comfortable and safe; attracts more guests; and shows staff are professional. Keeping rooms clean benefits guests, the hotel's business, and your reputation as a worker.

question: Which TWO things will give guests a good impression of you as a hotel worker?
options: Thick and colourful makeup + Clean and neat uniform
Thick and colourful makeup + Dirty fingernails
Clean and neat uniform + Dirty fingernails
Clean and neat uniform + Clean shaven
correctAnswer: Clean and neat uniform + Clean shaven
hint: A professional appearance is neat and clean. Heavy makeup and dirty nails are not professional.
explanation: A clean and neat uniform and being clean shaven give guests a good professional impression. Thick colourful makeup and dirty fingernails are not appropriate for a hotel worker and create a bad impression.

question: Which of the following are standard bed types found in a hotel room?
options: Bunk bed and single bed
Twin beds and double bed
Sofa bed and futon
Floor mattress and bunk bed
correctAnswer: Twin beds and double bed
hint: Standard hotel beds are proper beds guests sleep in. Bunk beds, sofa beds, and floor mattresses are not standard hotel room beds.
explanation: Standard hotel beds are twin beds (two single beds in one room) and double beds (one large bed). Bunk beds, sofa beds, futons, and floor mattresses are not standard hotel room bed types.

question: A guest left their keycard inside the room and cannot get in. What should you do?
options: Help the guest by opening the door to the room
Give the guest your keycard to open the door
Politely tell the guest you cannot open the door because you are very busy with work
Politely tell the guest you cannot open the door because you need to protect their safety and security
correctAnswer: Politely tell the guest you cannot open the door because you need to protect their safety and security
hint: You cannot verify that this person is really the guest. Opening the door for an unverified person is a safety risk.
explanation: Politely explain that you cannot open the door because you need to protect the guest's safety and security. You cannot confirm the person's identity. Direct them to the front desk who can verify their booking.

question: I am NOT allowed to open the door for a guest because I ___
options: Am not a friend of the guest
Did not seek permission from my supervisor
Am not able to open the door with my keycard
Do not have the guest's information to confirm their identity
correctAnswer: Do not have the guest's information to confirm their identity
hint: The key reason is about safety — you cannot prove the person is actually the guest who booked that room.
explanation: You are not allowed to open the door because you do not have the guest's information to confirm their identity. Anyone could claim to be the guest — protecting the real guest's safety means directing people to the front desk.

question: A guest needs help opening their room door. Where should you direct them?
options: Bellboy
Front desk
Restaurant manager
Your supervisor
correctAnswer: Front desk
hint: Who has the guest's booking information and can verify their identity?
explanation: Direct the guest to the front desk. The front desk has the guest's booking information and can verify their identity before granting access to the room. This protects the guest's safety and security.

question: What is the correct safety posture when making the bed?
options: Sit on the bed
Kneel on the bed
Kneel on the carpet
Stand and bend over the bed
correctAnswer: Kneel on the carpet
hint: This posture protects your back and keeps the bed clean. Think about where your knees should go.
explanation: Kneeling on the carpet is the correct safety posture when making the bed. It protects your back from injury and keeps the bed clean. Never sit or kneel ON the bed as it dirties and damages it.

question: What is the CORRECT work sequence when cleaning guestrooms and serviced apartments?
options: Make bed → Pack caddy → Clean bathroom → Vacuum room → Dust room
Vacuum room → Make bed → Pack caddy → Dust room → Clean bathroom
Pack caddy → Clean bathroom → Dust room → Make bed → Vacuum room
Pack caddy → Make bed → Dust room → Clean bathroom → Vacuum room
correctAnswer: Pack caddy → Make bed → Dust room → Clean bathroom → Vacuum room
hint: Start by preparing your tools (caddy). Work from top to bottom. Always vacuum LAST.
explanation: The correct sequence is: Pack caddy (prepare tools first) → Make bed → Dust room → Clean bathroom → Vacuum room. Packing the caddy first ensures you have everything you need. Vacuuming last means you don't dirty the floor again with later tasks.

question: Why is it important to vacuum the carpet LAST when servicing guestrooms? (TWO reasons)
options: Supervisors will be proud + Colleagues won't need to clean it again
Colleagues won't clean again + I won't dirty the carpet doing other tasks
I won't dirty the carpet doing other tasks + I can't walk and leave footprints after vacuuming
Supervisors will be proud + I can't walk and leave footprints after vacuuming
correctAnswer: I won't dirty the carpet doing other tasks + I can't walk and leave footprints after vacuuming
hint: Think about what happens to a freshly vacuumed carpet if you keep working or walking on it.
explanation: Vacuum last for two reasons: (1) You won't dirty the carpet by performing other cleaning tasks after vacuuming, and (2) you should not walk on or leave footprints on a freshly vacuumed carpet. Always vacuum as your very last step and then leave the room.

question: To create a pleasant environment in the room during turndown service, you will ___
options: Switch on both bedside lights + Replace the used guest amenities
Switch on 1 bedside light + Replace the used guest amenities
Switch on 1 bedside light + Set the air-con temperature to 25°C
Switch on both bedside lights + Set the air-con temperature to 25°C
correctAnswer: Switch on 1 bedside light + Set the air-con temperature to 25°C
hint: Turndown service creates a soft, comfortable sleeping atmosphere. Think: gentle light, comfortable temperature.
explanation: For a pleasant turndown environment, switch on ONE bedside light (creates a soft, relaxing glow — not both lights) and set the air-con to 25°C for a comfortable sleeping temperature.

question: What are TWO reasons for providing turndown service in hotels?
options: Guest can get ready for bed + Room will be ready for the next guest to check in
Guest can get ready for bed + Guest can relax and enjoy the evening before sleeping
Room ready for next guest + Guest will not need to make any requests to the hotel
Guest will not need to make requests + Guest can relax and enjoy the evening
correctAnswer: Guest can get ready for bed + Guest can relax and enjoy the evening before sleeping
hint: Turndown service is done for the CURRENT guest in the EVENING. It is NOT for the next guest.
explanation: Turndown service helps the current guest get ready for bed and relax and enjoy their evening before sleeping. It is NOT done to prepare the room for the next guest — that happens during regular servicing after checkout.

question: What is the LAST step when cleaning the kitchenette?
options: Wash the cutleries
Empty the trash bin
Sweep and mop the floor
Wipe the countertop, induction cooker and refrigerator
correctAnswer: Sweep and mop the floor
hint: Just like vacuuming the guestroom carpet, what should you always do LAST in the kitchenette?
explanation: Sweep and mop the floor is the LAST step when cleaning the kitchenette. You do the floor last so any dirt or water from washing cutleries and wiping surfaces falls to the floor first — then you clean the floor.

question: What is the CORRECT work sequence when cleaning the kitchenette?
options: Wash cutleries → Wipe equipment and counters → Mop floor → Remove rubbish
Wash cutleries → Remove rubbish → Mop floor → Wipe equipment and counters
Remove rubbish → Mop floor → Wash cutleries → Wipe equipment and counters
Remove rubbish → Wash cutleries → Wipe equipment and counters → Mop floor
correctAnswer: Remove rubbish → Wash cutleries → Wipe equipment and counters → Mop floor
hint: Start by clearing rubbish (remove waste first). Clean items and surfaces. Always mop the floor LAST.
explanation: The correct kitchenette sequence is: Remove rubbish → Wash cutleries → Wipe equipment and counters → Mop floor. Clear waste first, then clean items from top to bottom, and always mop the floor last.

question: Regarding guest privacy, which TWO statements are correct? I have to ___
options: Keep guest info confidential + Gossip about guests with colleagues
Keep guest info confidential + Protect privacy by not sharing info with others
Share guest medical conditions with supervisor + Gossip about guests
Share guest medical conditions with supervisor + Protect privacy
correctAnswer: Keep guest info confidential + Protect privacy by not sharing info with others
hint: Guest information is completely private. A professional hotel worker never shares or gossips about guests.
explanation: You must keep the guest's information confidential AND protect their privacy by not sharing guest information with other guests or colleagues. Gossiping about guests is unprofessional and violates their privacy and trust.

question: When hotel staff respect and protect the privacy of guests, the guests will ___
options: Not come back to the hotel
Trust the hotel and the staff
Have their information leaked
Not recommend the hotel to their friends
correctAnswer: Trust the hotel and the staff
hint: When guests feel their privacy is respected, how do they feel about the hotel and its staff?
explanation: When hotel staff respect and protect guest privacy, guests will trust the hotel and the staff. This trust leads to guests returning to the hotel and recommending it to others.

question: Why is it important to seek feedback from your supervisors? (TWO reasons)
options: My colleagues are doing it + I can improve skills I did not do well
I can improve skills I did not do well + Build skills and become a professional employee
Build skills and become professional + Tell supervisor I don't want their feedback
My colleagues are doing it + Tell supervisor I don't want their feedback
correctAnswer: I can improve skills I did not do well + Build skills and become a professional employee
hint: Feedback helps you grow. Think about what YOU personally gain from hearing feedback.
explanation: Seeking feedback is important because you can improve skills you did not do well, and build more skills to become a professional employee. Asking for feedback shows you are committed to doing your job well and growing in your role.

question: When seeking feedback from supervisors, I can ___ and ___
options: Learn which staff has resigned + Learn how many newcomers joined the team
Learn which staff resigned + Learn to provide better services for guests
Learn how many newcomers joined + Gain more housekeeping knowledge from supervisors
Learn to provide better professional services for guests + Gain more housekeeping knowledge
correctAnswer: Learn to provide better professional services for guests + Gain more housekeeping knowledge
hint: Focus on what YOU learn to improve YOUR own work — not information about other staff.
explanation: When seeking feedback, you can learn to provide better and more professional services for your guests, and gain more housekeeping knowledge when supervisors share information with you. Feedback is about improving your own skills and performance.

question: How can you find out more about new cleaning tools and equipment to help in your job?
options: Search the internet only
Attend exhibitions and fairs only
Read newspapers and magazines only
All of the above
correctAnswer: All of the above
hint: Is there just one way to learn about new tools, or are there many ways?
explanation: You can learn about new cleaning tools and equipment through all methods: searching the internet, attending exhibitions and fairs, and reading newspapers and magazines. Always look for ways to keep learning and improving your skills on the job.
